entries.value: Gross margin = (revenue - COGS) / revenue * 100. Target >50% for service businesses, >30% for product businesses. Declining gross margin signals rising input costs or pricing pressure. Review monthly by product line and cost center.

Net profit margin = net income / total revenue * 100. Target >10% for healthy businesses. Below 5% is fragile. Separate operating expenses from one-time items for accurate trending. Report on both GAAP and adjusted basis for management visibility.

Cash flow runway = current cash balance / monthly net burn rate. Target >6 months. Below 3 months is critical and requires immediate action. Calculate burn rate as the 3-month rolling average of net cash outflow. Present to leadership monthly.

AR days = (accounts receivable / annual revenue) * 365. Target <30 days. Above 45 days signals collection problems. Review aging buckets weekly: current, 1-30 days, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days. Escalate accounts over 60 days to collections process.

Budget variance = (actual - budgeted) / budgeted * 100. Target <5% absolute variance per department per period. Variance above 10% requires a written explanation from the budget owner. Track favorable (underspend) separately from unfavorable (overspend).

Fraud detection rate = fraud signals confirmed as actual fraud / total fraud that occurred. Target >95% detected within 24 hours. Track false positive rate separately - above 20% means the detection rules are too broad and eroding trust in alerts. Review detection rules quarterly.

Alert when any receivable invoice is overdue by >7 days. Escalate to account owner at 15 days. Flag for collections review at 45 days. At 90 days, evaluate write-off or collections referral. Send automated payment reminders at 7, 14, and 28 days past due.

Alert budget owner when spend reaches 75% of allocation before 75% of the period has elapsed. Critical alert at 95% spend. Require explicit CFO approval before any budget overrun is permitted. Never allow automated overspend.

Fraud signals with risk score >70 require immediate investigation within 4 hours. Risk score 50-70 requires review within 24 hours. Risk score below 50 can be batched for weekly review. Transactions above $50,000 always require manual review regardless of score.

Month-end close should complete within 5 business days. Week 1: reconcile all accounts. Week 2: prepare management reports. Hard close on day 5. Any journal entry after day 5 requires VP Finance approval. Maintain a close checklist with owner and due date for each step.

Send invoices within 24 hours of service delivery or billing period end. Use Net 30 terms as default for new customers. Offer 2% discount for payment within 10 days on invoices above $10,000. Always include a unique invoice number and bank transfer details. Automate reminders.

Gross burn rate = total monthly operating expenses. Net burn rate = total expenses minus total revenue. A profitable business has negative net burn (positive cash flow). Track both to separate growth investment from operational efficiency. Investors typically ask for net burn.

All expenses above $500 require manager approval before incurrence. Expenses above $5,000 require VP or CFO approval. Travel and entertainment must be submitted with receipts within 30 days. Personal expenses submitted as business expenses are a terminable offense per company policy.
